personality_one_liner: A data-dense, hyper-glanceable finance command center where metrics are the heroes and clarity beats aesthetic.

spacing.rule: Tight, data-dense spacing. Avoid accidental empty space; use md/lg padding structurally for clear horizontal scan lines.

do's: Bottom tab bar (heavy tint, med blur)

screens.name: Markets
screens.purpose: Glanceable overview of US and Indian market indices and top movers.

states.loading: Skeleton rows mapping the horizontal index cards and vertical movers list.
states.empty: No data available state with a 'Refresh' button.
states.error: Failed to load market data, standard retry view.

screens.layout: Top section: Sticky header with an inline Segmented Control (US / India) toggle. Below: A horizontally scrollable row of 'Index Cards' (S&P 500, Nasdaq / Nifty 50, Sensex) displaying the value, change %, and a micro-sparkline. Below that: 'Top Gainers' and 'Top Losers' list. List items use a dense layout: Ticker (left), Sparkline (middle, using success/error color), Current Price + Change (right, right-aligned).

screens.name: Radar
screens.purpose: Surface pre-built sophisticated screening strategies as actionable 'scans'.

states.empty: No scans available, show an abstract radar icon with 'System updating'.
states.error: Failed to load radar signals, retry button.

screens.layout: Header with 'Active Scans' title. Layout is a 2-column grid (`flexWrap: wrap`, `justifyContent: space-between`). Each card is a 'Strategy' (e.g., 'Momentum Breakouts', 'High ROCE'). Cards have a dark gradient or abstract background (image slot), an icon, a title, and a bottom badge (e.g., '24 Stocks').

screens.name: Screener
screens.purpose: Deep custom filtering to find specific stocks across multiple metrics.

states.loading: Skeleton filter chips and list rows.
states.empty: Illustration of an empty box/grid: 'No stocks match your filters. Try broadening your criteria.'

screens.layout: Top sticky area: Horizontally scrollable row of `surfaceTertiary` pill chips representing active filters (e.g., 'P/E < 20', 'Market Cap > 5B'). Content: Vertical list of stocks. Each row displays the company, ticker, price, and highlighted badges showing the specific filtered metrics. A Floating Action Button (FAB) at the bottom right to 'Add Filter' which opens a full-screen Bottom Sheet with categorized sliders/inputs (Fundamentals, Technicals, Performance).

screens.name: Watchlist
screens.purpose: Locally persisted list of user-tracked stocks.

states.empty: Illustration of floating cubes, text 'Your watchlist is empty. Start adding stocks from the Screener.', CTA 'Go to Screener'.

screens.layout: Simple dense list of saved stocks. Stock rows match the Home 'Market Movers' pattern (Ticker, Sparkline, Price/Change). Top right header action to Edit/Delete.

screens.name: Stock Detail
screens.purpose: Comprehensive, data-dense view of a single stock's fundamentals and technicals.
screens.layout: Glass sticky header with Ticker + Price (appears on scroll). Hero area: Large interactive price chart (candlestick or line). Below chart: Segmented control for timeframe (1D, 1W, 1M, 1Y). Next section: 'Key Metrics' presented in a dense 2x2 or 3x2 grid of small cards (P/E, Mkt Cap, Div Yield, ROE). Followed by 'Technicals' (RSI, MACD indicators in progress bars or badges).

radar_card_bg.alt: Abstract wavy lines for Radar card background

empty_watchlist.alt: Floating cubes abstract illustration for empty state

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the '7 Dark-First Utility DARK' personality. Do NOT use blues or purples anywhere. Green and Red are functional status colors for gains and losses.
The app does not have trading functionalities. Do not add Buy/Sell buttons. Focus entirely on data visualization, filtering, and screening.
Rely heavily on SpaceGrotesk for numeric data and display elements, and IBMPlexSans for readable body text.
Implement dense, glanceable list items for stocks. Ticker on the left, an inline micro-chart (sparkline) in the middle, and right-aligned Price + Change %.
Use solid `surfaceSecondary` for most cards. Only use glassmorphism on the bottom tab bar and sticky headers to maintain performance and utility.
For the Radar pre-built screens, use a 2-column grid wrapped gracefully (`justifyContent: space-between`). The cards should use `radar_card_bg` as a subtle overlay with a dark scrim.
Ensure all text rendered on top of imagery uses a top-to-bottom dark gradient scrim to guarantee WCAG contrast compliance.
